evidence_summary: Using character-level information can improve vector representations for morphologically rich languages.
Representing words as the sum of character n-gram vectors can be an effective approach.
Replacing the embedding layer with a parametrized neural network that processes characters of a given word allows the model to consume arbitrary words and is not restricted to a fixed vocabulary.
Encoding rare and unknown words as sequences of subword units can make NMT models capable of open-vocabulary translation.
